title: Cow<str> in expand_vars: zero-alloc fast path is correct and should be kept
description: expand_vars (evaluator.rs:686) uses Cow<'a, str> and returns Cow::Borrowed(s) when no substitution occurs. This means the common case (no variables, or a string with no dollar signs) pays zero allocation cost. A reviewer might suggest 'just return String for simplicity.' That would be wrong: evaluate() is called per-message, and most strings in typical Sieve scripts (header names, literal keys) never need substitution. The Cow choice is precisely correct here and should be defended.
